name: Thyme-Roasted Chicken Breast w/ Morel-Madeira
recipeIngredient: 1 oz Dried morels, rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine boiling water and morels in a small bowl. Let stand until morels soften, about 35 minutes.
2. Strain; reserve soaking liquid. Coarsely chop the morels.
3. Preheat oven to 400°F. Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
4. Add chicken to skillet; cook until golden, about 4 minutes on each side. Transfer chicken to a shallow baking dish (do not clean skillet).
5. Bake until chicken is cooked through, about 15 minutes.
6. Meanwhile, heat the same skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion, garlic, and thyme; sauté until golden, about 5 minutes.
7. Add morel soaking liquid, leaving any residue in the bowl. Bring to a boil and reduce the mixture until most of the liquid evaporates, about 4 minutes.
8. Add chicken broth and 1/4 cup Madeira; bring to a boil until gravy is reduced to 1 1/2 cups, about 5 minutes.
9. Strain gravy into a small saucepan; add morels. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
10. Whisk 1 tablespoon cornstarch and 1 tbsp Madeira in a small bowl. Whisk into gravy; simmer until thickened, about 30 seconds.
11. Season gravy with salt and pepper. Spoon over chicken and serve.
